title: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability
display_title: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability
wikitext:
[]: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Sat Jun 19, 2021 9:38 pm AKA: I have no idea what those squiggly lines mean, can I still play this game? I've only played a few EVA games but regardless of quality, I'd rank em like this- Fully playable: Battle Orchestra/Portable (PS2/PSP) 3nd Impact (PSP) EVA Dawn (Mobile, actually has an official, if very jank, English translation) Angel Raising Project/Shito Ikusei (WonderSwan, one of the rare EVA games with a fan translation) A bit tricky: Neon Genesis Evangelion (N64) Neon Genesis Evangelion 2nd Impression (Saturn) Difficult: Neon Genesis Evangelion [1st Impression] (Saturn) Impossible: Evangelion Jo (PS2/PSP) Neon Genesis Evangelion 2 (PS2/PSP)

[]: Re: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Jun 20, 2021 12:07 pm NGE2 and I think also Jo have at least partially translated playthroughs on youtube, so if you just want to experience the story, not all is lost. And, from personal experience, Dawn's UI is intuitive enough that even knowing fuckall of the language it is perfectly playable in basically all of the modes

[]: Re: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Jun 22, 2021 12:49 am Girlfriend of Steel 1, Girlfriend of Steel 2, Ayanami Raising Project, Petit Eva and Shinji ikari Raising Project have fan translations too. IIRC only Petit Eva runs on an emulator (Nitendo DS), the rest have PC versions, but I'd say only GoS 1 is worth your time... 1st Impression actually has a DVD version, and it was machine-translated here: thread/20831/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-EpisodeX-25-minute-OVA/ Ditto for 2nd impression, same channel. One guy told me the machine translation was unreliable. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xUUydpmCnlQ&t=1875s NGE2 is still being translated, and Reichu did those English play-throughs Blockio mentioned.

[]: Re: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Tue Jun 22, 2021 3:47 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: NGE2 is still being translated, and Reichu did those English play-throughs Blockio mentioned.

[]: Re: EVA games ranked by filthy gaijin playability             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon Jun 28, 2021 11:50 am Are you counting crossover games and such too? Because one of the Super Robot Wars games (Super Robot Wars V) featured Rebuild and got an official English language release in...Taiwan I think it was. There are a few other SRW games with Eva that are Japan only but have guides and such online that translate their entire story.
